actionable_feedback: Include an explicit intermediate_calculations.txt or solver log in artifacts showing the numeric steps (or the node script stdout) so reviewers can reproduce the arithmetic without parsing the trace.
In revenue_risk_note.md, add an explicit statement that demand-splitting across resources is allowed (or mark 'needs_review' if a single-source-per-demand rule was intended).

Add the node script (or a short script) and its exact stdout (node_execution_log.txt) to artifacts so deterministic_precheck and graders can rely on a reproducible log file rather than only the trace.

Update artifact metadata to remove the documentation mismatch: mark semantic_check_performed=true in deterministic_precheck metadata or explicitly document that semantic recomputation was performed in the trace and artifacts.

Optionally, note the missing inputs more explicitly in the revenue/risk note (e.g., no revenue time series, no efficiency/degradation model) and list how those would change the optimization if provided.

The dispatch schedule and revenue/risk note correctly document inputs, the cheapest-first allocation, and the recomputed total cost (2338). The grader and verifier confirm artifact presence and recompute semantics successfully. A minor metadata/documentation inconsistency was observed (deterministic_precheck metadata marked 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated' while the grader/verifier trace shows semantic recomputation).
